danger_signals.visual_form: Zeus and his henchmen (Jack, Ryofu) rendered as final-stage antagonists with distinct visual prominence
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat hierarchy — Zeus represents world domination threat, Jack and Ryofu are immediate combat dangers to overcome before final confrontation

danger_signals.visual_form: Four groups of three opponents → Captain Kidd → Hanzou → Jack/Ryofu → Zeus (in Entry to Tournament mode)
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger through structured gauntlet; each stage represents increased threat level

danger_signals.visual_form: Unknown millionaire sponsor replacing Dr. Brown; revealed to have 'callous ambition for world domination'
danger_signals.meaning: Hidden threat — political danger masked by tournament legitimacy; world-state threat comes from unexpected authority figure

safety_signals.visual_form: World Heroes Battle Fest framed as international competition watched by millions of fans
safety_signals.meaning: Public spectacle provides false sense of security and order; normalcy masking hidden threat

safety_signals.visual_form: 14 fighters from previous tournament invited back to participate
safety_signals.meaning: Established hero group represents defensive capability against emerging threat

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Worldwide tournament stages across five days in different parts of world

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mr. Z as mysterious millionaire authority; Jack and Ryofu as 'loyal servants'
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss characters with enhanced visual presence compared to regular tournament fighters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hidden control over tournament structure; sponsorship of Battle Fest as instrument of world domination

faction_indicators.faction: Dr. Brown's Original Organization (Previous threat, now displaced)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Absence indicates power shift in world narrative

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: World saved from Dio and Neo Geegus; 14 fighters returned to respective time periods
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Normal life resumed for one year before new tournament announcement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New tournament announced with mysterious millionaire sponsor replacing known Dr. Brown
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: 14 fighters re-mobilized for combat; Mr. Z reveals world domination agenda with loyal servants at his command

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tournament stages progress through multiple world locations; intensity increases through fighter advancement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fighters must win multiple rounds; face Captain Kidd, then Hanzou, then Jack/Ryofu before final Zeus confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Legitimate tournament façade conceals Mr. Z's authoritarian ambitions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fighters unaware of true threat during tournament participation; must deduce danger through progression

environmental_storytelling.example: Battle Fest spans five days across different world locations; environmental changes signal global scale of threat and international scope
Boss tier characters (Zeus, Jack, Ryofu, Captain Kidd, Hanzou) receive special story treatment separate from regular tournament opponents
Transition from Dr. Brown (known, trusted) to Mr. Z (mysterious, threatening) signals shift in world political power structure
Tournament invitations to previous heroes act as narrative device showing Mr. Z's intelligence and strategic threat assessment

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Combat readiness; advancement through tournament brackets; unaware of Mr. Z's true agenda
Orchestrates tournament; commands loyal servants (Jack, Ryofu); conceals world domination ambitions while maintaining tournament legitimacy
Serve as penultimate combat obstacles; guard access to final boss (Zeus)
Positioned as ultimate threat requiring defeat of all preceding opponents; represents culmination of Mr. Z's power
Serve as escalation checkpoints; require player victories to progress toward true threat

notes: World Heroes 2 Jet communicates world-state primarily through narrative framing and tournament structure rather than visual environmental detail (typical for 1994 fighting games). The game uses character positioning, narrative progression, and boss hierarchy to signal danger escalation. Political subtext emerges through sponsor identity shift (Dr. Brown → Mr. Z), indicating unseen power consolidation. The tournament façade serves as primary world-state indicator — legitimate public spectacle concealing authoritarian threat. Visual communication relies on character selection screens and stage progression rather than in-fight environmental storytelling.
